title: TCP/IP Protocol Suite Layers and Encapsulation
description: A visual representation of the four-layer TCP/IP model, showing the flow of data from an application down through the protocol stack, the encapsulation process where each layer adds its own header, and the corresponding protocols at each layer.
relationships: Data flows downward from Application to Network Access for transmission, with each layer encapsulating the payload from the layer above by adding its own header.
On the receiving end, data flows upward, with each layer processing and removing its respective header before passing the payload to the layer above.
